Longitudinal O*NET archive analysis

Source: vignettes/longitudinal-archives.Rmd
longitudinal-archives.Rmd
library(onet2r)
library(tibble)

The longitudinal helpers support three related workflows:

  1. Discover available O*NET database releases.
  2. Read descriptor tables from archived text files into a consistent panel.
  3. Reconcile adjacent releases and separate real updates from carryforwards, resampling, recodes, and crosswalk uncertainty.

Live archive calls are guarded because they download files from O*NET. In an interactive session, start by listing releases and reading a descriptor table:

releases <- onet_releases()
head(releases)

abilities <- onet_archive_read("30.3", "Abilities")
head(abilities)

panel <- onet_panel("Abilities", versions = c("30.2", "30.3"), scale = "IM")

For reproducible examples and tests, the reconciliation step can use any tibble with the same schema returned by onet_panel(). This tiny panel contains four occupation-element pairs across two releases. Each pair demonstrates one branch of the change classification truth table.

panel <- tibble(
  release_version = rep(c("1.0", "2.0"), each = 4),
  release_date = rep(as.Date(c("2020-01-01", "2021-01-01")), each = 4),
  soc_vintage = "2019",
  domain = "Abilities",
  onet_soc_code = rep(
    c("11-1011.00", "15-1252.00", "29-1141.00", "41-1011.00"),
    2
  ),
  soc_code = rep(c("11-1011", "15-1252", "29-1141", "41-1011"), 2),
  title = "Occupation",
  element_id = "1.A.1.a.1",
  element_name = "Oral Comprehension",
  scale_id = "IM",
  data_value = c(4, 4, 4, 4, 4, 5, 4, 5),
  source_date = c(
    as.Date(c("2020-01-01", "2020-01-01", "2020-01-01", "2020-01-01")),
    as.Date(c("2020-01-01", "2021-01-01", "2021-01-01", "2020-01-01"))
  ),
  domain_source = "Analyst"
)

bridge <- tibble(
  from_vintage = "2019",
  to_vintage = "2019",
  from_soc_code = c("11-1011", "15-1252", "29-1141", "41-1011"),
  to_soc_code = c("11-1011", "15-1252", "29-1141", "41-1011"),
  from_title = "Occupation",
  to_title = "Occupation",
  step_count = 0L,
  map_type = "one_to_one",
  crosswalk_weight = 1
)

changes <- onet_panel_reconcile(panel, bridge)
changes[, c(
  "from_soc_code",
  "to_soc_code",
  "value_change",
  "date_changed",
  "change_type",
  "safely_comparable"
)]
#> # A tibble: 4 × 6
#>   from_soc_code to_soc_code value_change date_changed change_type          
#>   <chr>         <chr>              <dbl> <lgl>        <fct>                
#> 1 11-1011       11-1011                0 FALSE        stale_carryforward   
#> 2 15-1252       15-1252                1 TRUE         real_update          
#> 3 29-1141       29-1141                0 TRUE         resampled_stable     
#> 4 41-1011       41-1011                1 FALSE        recode_or_recalc_flag
#> # ℹ 1 more variable: safely_comparable <lgl>

The truth table is:

value changed source date changed change_type initially comparable
no no stale_carryforward yes
yes yes real_update yes
no yes resampled_stable yes
yes no recode_or_recalc_flag no

method_break and crosswalk_uncertain are override flags. A row is safely comparable only when the truth-table result is comparable and neither override flag is present.

onet_change_summary(changes, by = "job_family")
#> # A tibble: 5 × 10
#>   summary_level job_family change_type     n_pairs share_pairs mean_value_change
#>   <chr>         <chr>      <chr>             <int>       <dbl>             <dbl>
#> 1 overall       NA         stale_carryfor…       4        1                  0.5
#> 2 job_family    11         stale_carryfor…       1        0.25               0  
#> 3 job_family    15         real_update           1        0.25               1  
#> 4 job_family    29         resampled_stab…       1        0.25               0  
#> 5 job_family    41         recode_or_reca…       1        0.25               1  
#> # ℹ 4 more variables: median_abs_value_change <dbl>,
#> #   share_safely_comparable <dbl>, share_method_break <dbl>,
#> #   share_crosswalk_uncertain <dbl>

When comparing across taxonomy vintages, create a bridge first:

bridge_2010_2019 <- onet_crosswalk_bridge("2010", "2019")
table(bridge_2010_2019$map_type)

Rows with map_type equal to split or merge are retained, but crosswalk_uncertain is set to TRUE during reconciliation. This lets users keep the data while avoiding overconfident longitudinal claims.

Developed by Alex Farach.

Site built with pkgdown 2.1.3.
